title: Systems, devices, and methods for RF detection of analyte sensor measurements
abstract: Embodiments that translate a sensor measurement to a frequency characteristic are disclosed. The frequency characteristic can be wirelessly detected by a reader device. The detected frequency characteristic can be used to determine the corresponding sensor measurement. Devices utilizing this approach can be characterized or calibrated to increase accuracy. Systems and methods utilizing the approaches are also described.
claims: 1. A method of detecting an in vivo glucose level, comprising: outputting, by an electrochemical glucose sensor of a first device, an electrical current or voltage that corresponds to the in vivo glucose level, wherein the electrochemical glucose sensor is electrically coupled with a circuit having a frequency characteristic configured to vary according to the electrical current or voltage, and wherein the electrical current or voltage is configured to vary according to the in vivo glucose level; wirelessly detecting the frequency characteristic of the circuit with a second device; and determining the in vivo glucose level based on the detected frequency characteristic.
